title: Running podman under systemd DynamicUser
body:
Nesting podman doesn't work. We know that. But I still need to isolate an emepheral service which runs containers. So I'm trying systemd's `DynamicUser` facility. I didn't expect it to be painless. Okay, here's what I have so far:

1. I start off by running bash under `DynamicUser`:

```
# systemd-run -p DynamicUser=1 -p Environment=HOME=/run/foo -p RuntimeDirectory=foo -p WorkingDirectory=/run/foo --wait -t /bin/bash
```

2. Next I run `podman`:

```
$ podman run --rm -it debian
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 64663` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
ERRO[0000] cannot find UID/GID for user run-u47: No subuid ranges found for user "run-u47" in /etc/subuid - check rootless mode in man pages. 
WARN[0000] using rootless single mapping into the namespace. This might break some images. Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid for adding sub*ids 
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 64663` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
Resolved "debian" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/debian:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 647acf3d48c2 done  
Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:647acf3d48c2780e00cd27bb0984367415f270d78477ef9d5b238e6ebd5290da": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): open /bin/mount: operation not permitted
WARN[0003] Failed to add pause process to systemd sandbox cgroup: dbus: couldn't determine address of session bus 
bash-5.1$ getenforce 
```

The `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid` problem I expected. But I didn't expect the rest. Is there any hope here?

comments.body:
`DynamicUser=1` doesn't create a systemd session for the ephemeral user, so there are some limitations even if we manage to get it running.

Since you are running with a single ID, you will at least need `--storage-opt overlay.ignore_chown_errors=true`.

The error you are seeing is because systems mounts the `WorkingDirectory` with `nosuid`:

The issue you are seeing though seems to be caused by `RestrictSUIDSGID=` that is implied with `DynamicUser=1`:

```
Furthermore NoNewPrivileges= and RestrictSUIDSGID= are implicitly enabled (and cannot be disabled), to ensure that processes invoked cannot take benefit or create SUID/SGID files or directories. 
```

So there is nothing we can do from Podman to run in such a restricted environment.
